title.zh: 形容词（前置、后置、简单、复合、比较级、最高级）

summary.en: Adjectives describe people and things. They work as attributive, subject complement, object complement, and adverbial; they sit before or after the noun, follow a strict ordering, form compounds, and inflect into comparative and superlative degrees.
summary.zh: 形容词用来描述人和物。它们可作定语、表语、宾语补语和状语；可前置或后置，前置时有固定顺序，可构成复合形容词，并能变成比较级和最高级。

heading.zh: 前置定语形容词

body.en: The simplest, most direct use is to put the adjective right before the thing it describes. In 'a hungry rabbit', 'hungry' restricts and describes the noun — it is an attributive. An adjective acting as an attributive usually goes before its noun, which is called a pre-positioned attributive.
body.zh: 最简单直接的用法就是把形容词直接放在要描述的对象之前。如 a hungry rabbit，hungry 起限定描述作用，是定语。这样作定语的形容词一般放在要描述对象之前，叫前置定语。

concepts.sourceRef: 这个形容词hungry直接起限定描述作用，所以是定语

heading.zh: 多个前置形容词的顺序

body.en: When several adjectives without hyphens stack before a noun, order matters. 'A little white rabbit' sounds right; 'a white little rabbit' is understandable but sounds odd. The usual order is: determiner + opinion/subjective + size + shape + age (new/old) + colour + origin + material + purpose.
body.zh: 当多个不加连字符的形容词作前置定语时，顺序很重要。a little white rabbit 听上去自然，a white little rabbit 意思没问题却怪怪的。一般顺序是：限定词 + 主观特征 + 尺寸 + 形状 + 新旧 + 颜色 + 来源 + 材料 + 用途。

concepts.sourceRef: 限定词，也就是冠词、物主代词等，加主观特征，加尺寸加形状，加新旧，加颜色加来源，加材料加用途

heading.zh: 后置定语：复合不定代词

body.en: In some cases an adjective can only follow the noun (a post-positioned attributive). When it modifies a compound indefinite pronoun such as somebody, anybody, everything, or nothing, it must come after: 'somebody nice', 'anybody absent', 'everything possible', 'nothing wrong'.
body.zh: 某些情况下形容词只能后置作定语，即后置定语。修饰复合不定代词 somebody, anybody, everything, nothing 等时只能后置：somebody nice、anybody absent、everything possible、nothing wrong。

concepts.sourceRef: 用形容词修饰复合不定代词，somebody, anybody, everything, nothing等时，咱们只能说somebody nice

heading.zh: 后置定语：形容词短语

body.en: An adjective phrase is generally placed after the thing it describes, e.g. 'carrots good for the rabbit'. Phrases built with non-finite verbs work the same way: 'the rabbit eager to eat carrots' (adjective + infinitive) and 'the rabbit busy eating carrots' (adjective + gerund). Each can be rewritten as a relative clause: 'the rabbit that is eager to eat carrots'.
body.zh: 形容词短语一般放在描述对象之后，如 carrots good for the rabbit。用非谓语动词构成的短语同理：the rabbit eager to eat carrots（形容词加不定式）、the rabbit busy eating carrots（形容词加动名词）。它们都可用定语从句替换，如 the rabbit that is eager to eat carrots。

concepts.sourceRef: 一般说来，形容词短语会放在描述的对象之后，比如carrots good for the rabbit

heading.en: Subject complement (predicative) after a linking verb
heading.zh: 系动词后的主语补语（表语）

body.en: A linking verb connects what comes before it with what comes after, giving the latter's quality to the former. In 'the rabbit is hungry', 'is' is a linking verb: it gives the quality 'hungry' to the subject 'the rabbit'. The adjective after it supplements the subject — the subject complement, also called the predicative (表语).
body.zh: 系动词把它前后联系起来，把后者的性质赋予前者。the rabbit is hungry 中 is 是系动词，把 hungry 这个性质赋予主语 the rabbit。它后面的形容词补充说明主语，就是主语补语，又叫表语。

concepts.sourceRef: 把后者表达的性质赋予前者

heading.zh: 只作表语的形容词（a-前缀、身体状况）

body.en: Some adjectives normally work only as predicatives. Many beginning with the prefix 'a-', like alive, asleep, alone, can only say 'the rabbit is alive/asleep/alone', not 'an alive rabbit'. For an attributive you switch words: a living, sleeping, lonely rabbit. Some health adjectives like ill and well behave the same — 'the rabbit is ill/well', not 'an ill rabbit'; use a sick or healthy rabbit instead.
body.zh: 有些形容词通常只作表语。许多以前缀 a- 开头的，如 alive, asleep, alone，只能说 the rabbit is alive/asleep/alone，不能说 an alive rabbit。想作定语要换词：a living, sleeping, lonely rabbit。某些表身体状况的如 ill, well 同理，只能说 the rabbit is ill/well，作定语要换成 a sick, healthy rabbit。

concepts.sourceRef: 有些英语形容词通常只做表语，比如一些以这个前缀开头的形容词，像是 alive, asleep, alone

heading.zh: 宾语补语与状语用法

body.en: Beyond attributive and predicative, an adjective can be an object complement: in 'carrots make rabbits happy', 'happy' supplements the object 'rabbits'. More advanced still, an adjective phrase can act as an adverbial: in 'the rabbit came home tired and hungry', 'tired and hungry' shows the rabbit's state on arriving home.
body.zh: 除了定语和表语，形容词还能作宾语补语：carrots make rabbits happy 中 happy 补充宾语 rabbits 的状态。更高级的是作状语：the rabbit came home tired and hungry 中 tired and hungry 表现兔子回到家时的状态，作状语。

concepts.sourceRef: 形容词 happy 补充说明了宾语 rabbits 的状态，做宾语补语

heading.zh: 复合形容词：以形容词为首

body.en: Besides simple adjectives, there are compound adjectives built from several words. The easiest is adjective + adjective, e.g. 'a red hot coal'. Since present and past participles can act as adjectives, we also get adjective + present participle ('a good looking rabbit') and adjective + past participle ('a new born baby').
body.zh: 除了简单形容词，还有由多个词构成的复合形容词。最好理解的是形容词加形容词，如 a red hot coal。由于现在分词和过去分词可作形容词，于是有形容词加现在分词（a good looking rabbit）和形容词加过去分词（a new born baby）。

concepts.sourceRef: 形容词加形容词的构成是最好理解的，比如 a red hot coal

heading.zh: 复合形容词：以副词为首

body.en: An adverb can sit before an adjective to modify it, so adverb-based compounds are also easy: 'all round' (adverb + adjective), 'hard working' (adverb + present participle), 'recently built' (adverb + past participle).
body.zh: 副词可放在形容词前修饰它，所以以副词为首的复合形容词也不难：all round（副词加形容词）、hard working（副词加现在分词）、recently built（副词加过去分词）。

concepts.sourceRef: 副词也可以放在形容词前修饰形容词

heading.en: Compound adjectives: adjective + noun (= a clause)
heading.zh: 复合形容词：形容词加名词（相当于从句）

body.en: An adjective + noun compound is a bit special: it is equivalent to a relative clause. 'A white haired man' = 'a man whose hair is white'; 'a kind hearted woman' = 'a woman whose heart is kind'. The second word (the noun) usually takes an '-ed' ending — unrelated to the past tense. Because these use 'whose', they describe people or animals that can own such attributes.
body.zh: 形容词加名词的复合稍特殊：相当于定语从句。a white haired man = a man whose hair is white；a kind hearted woman = a woman whose heart is kind。第二个词（名词）通常加 -ed，与动词过去时无关。因为用 whose，这类形容词往往修饰有所有者属性的人或动物。

concepts.sourceRef: a white haired man 等于 a man whose hair is white

heading.zh: 复合形容词：以名词为首

body.en: Noun-first compounds follow the same clause-equivalent logic. 'A snow white rabbit' (noun + adjective) = 'a rabbit that is white like snow'; 'a carrot eating rabbit' (noun + present participle) = 'a rabbit that eats carrots'; 'a heartbroken rabbit' (noun + past participle) = 'a rabbit whose heart is broken'. Numbers behave like adjectives too, e.g. 'a one way street'.
body.zh: 以名词为首的复合形容词同样遵循从句等效逻辑。a snow white rabbit（名词加形容词）= a rabbit that is white like snow；a carrot eating rabbit（名词加现在分词）= a rabbit that eats carrots；a heartbroken rabbit（名词加过去分词）= a rabbit whose heart is broken。数字也像形容词，如 a one way street。

concepts.sourceRef: "a snow white rabbit"，名词加形容词，等于 "a rabbit that is white like snow"

heading.zh: 比较级与最高级：规则 -er / -est

body.en: Comparative and superlative degrees express 'more' and 'most'. The basic rule adds '-er' and '-est' to the adjective: long, longer, longest; tall, taller, tallest. Words ending in 'e' just add '-r' and '-st' (late, later, latest; large, larger, largest) to avoid mis-spelling and mis-reading.
body.zh: 比较级和最高级表达“更”和“最”。基本规则是在词尾加 -er 和 -est：long, longer, longest；tall, taller, tallest。以 e 结尾的词只加 -r 和 -st（late, later, latest；large, larger, largest），以防拼写出错导致读音出错。

concepts.sourceRef: 构成比较级和最高级形容词直接在形容词词尾加 "er" 和 "est" 就好

heading.zh: 拼写规则：-y、双写辅音

body.en: A word ending in consonant + 'y' changes 'y' to 'i' before '-er'/'-est': happy, happier, happiest; busy, busier, busiest. A stressed closed-syllable word doubles its final consonant first: big, bigger, biggest; fat, fatter, fattest. These spellings prevent mis-reading.
body.zh: 辅音字母加 y 结尾的词，把 y 变 i 再加 -er/-est：happy, happier, happiest；busy, busier, busiest。重读闭音节单词先双写词尾辅音字母再加：big, bigger, biggest；fat, fatter, fattest。这些拼写是为防止误读。

concepts.sourceRef: 辅音字母加 "y" 结尾的词，把 "y" 变 "i" 再加 "er" 和 "est"

heading.zh: 比较级与最高级：more / most

body.en: Multi-syllable words and some two-syllable words take 'more' and 'most' before the adjective instead: interesting, more interesting, most interesting; difficult, more difficult, most difficult. 'More beautiful' is the comparative of 'beautiful'; 'the fairest' is the superlative of 'fair'.
body.zh: 多音节词和部分双音节词在词前加 more 和 most：interesting, more interesting, most interesting；difficult, more difficult, most difficult。more beautiful 是 beautiful 的比较级；the fairest 是 fair 的最高级。

concepts.sourceRef: 多音节词和部分双音节词则是在词前加 "more" 和 "most"

heading.zh: 不规则变化

body.en: Some adjectives change irregularly: good, better, best; bad, worse, worst; many, more, most; little, less, least.
body.zh: 有些形容词不规则变化：good, better, best；bad, worse, worst；many, more, most；little, less, least。

concepts.sourceRef: 好更好最好

heading.zh: 比较级与 than 及倍数

body.en: The basic pattern is 'A is + comparative + than + B': 'this carrot is bigger than that one'. To show a multiple, add 'times' before the comparative: 'this carrot is three times bigger than that one'.
body.zh: 基本句型是“A is 比较级 than B”：this carrot is bigger than that one。表示倍数时在比较级前加 times：this carrot is three times bigger than that one。

concepts.sourceRef: this carrot is three times bigger than that one

heading.zh: 同级比较：as ... as

body.en: To say two things are equally something, use 'as + positive degree + as' — no comparative, just the base form: 'this carrot is as big as that one'. For a multiple, put 'times' before the first 'as': 'this carrot is three times as big as that one'.
body.zh: 表示两者一样时用“as 原级 as”——不用比较级，只用原级：this carrot is as big as that one。表示倍数时在第一个 as 前加 times：this carrot is three times as big as that one。

concepts.sourceRef: this carrot is as big as that one

heading.zh: “比较级 and 比较级”= 越来越

body.en: A repeated comparative expresses 'more and more'. For '-er' words, use comparative + 'and' + comparative: 'prettier and prettier', 'smarter and smarter'. For 'more' words, don't repeat the whole phrase — use 'more and more + adjective': 'more and more beautiful', not 'more beautiful and more beautiful'.
body.zh: 重复比较级表达“越来越”。对加 -er 的词用“比较级 and 比较级”：prettier and prettier、smarter and smarter。对用 more 的词不要整段重复，用“more and more 加形容词”：more and more beautiful，而非 more beautiful and more beautiful。

concepts.sourceRef: “more and more beautiful”，越来越漂亮

heading.zh: “the 比较级, the 比较级”句型

body.en: To say 'the more X (cause), the more Y (result)', use 'the + comparative + ..., the + comparative + ...': 'The more carrots the rabbit eats, the smarter he will become.' For a past fact, use past tense: 'the more carrots the rabbit ate, the smarter he became.' Context can shorten it: 'the more the better'.
body.zh: 表达“越……（原因），就越……（结果）”，用“the 比较级 ……, the 比较级 ……”：The more carrots the rabbit eats, the smarter he will become。描述过去事实用过去时：the more carrots the rabbit ate, the smarter he became。靠语境可省略：the more the better。

concepts.sourceRef: The more carrots the rabbit eats, the smarter he will become

heading.zh: 最高级：范围、the 与 a most

body.en: The superlative comes with a preset range, usually 'in + place' or 'of + group': 'who is the fairest one of all?' A superlative modifying a noun normally takes 'the' because there is only one: 'Snow White is the most beautiful woman in the world.' But 'a most beautiful woman' uses 'a' because here 'most' means 'very' — 'a most beautiful woman' = 'a very beautiful woman'.
body.zh: 最高级常和预设范围连用，通常是 in 加地点或 of 加所属：who is the fairest one of all。最高级修饰名词时一般加 the，因为只有一个：Snow White is the most beautiful woman in the world。但 a most beautiful woman 用 a，因为这里 most 表示“非常”——a most beautiful woman = a very beautiful woman。

concepts.sourceRef: 之所以用“the”，就是因为最高级只有一个

note.en: 'Hungry' is a pre-positioned attributive describing 'rabbit'.
note.zh: hungry 是前置定语，描述 rabbit。

examples.text: a beautiful big round new black Chinese wooden table

note.en: Determiner + opinion + size + shape + age + colour + origin + material + noun — the full adjective-order rainbow.
note.zh: 限定词 + 主观 + 尺寸 + 形状 + 新旧 + 颜色 + 来源 + 材料 + 名词——完整的形容词顺序彩虹。

note.en: With a compound indefinite pronoun, the adjective must follow — a post-positioned attributive.
note.zh: 修饰复合不定代词时形容词必须后置——后置定语。

note.en: An adjective phrase placed after the noun 'carrots'.
note.zh: 形容词短语放在名词 carrots 之后。

note.en: Linking verb 'is' gives 'hungry' to the subject; 'hungry' is the subject complement (predicative).
note.zh: 系动词 is 把 hungry 赋予主语；hungry 是主语补语（表语）。

note.en: 'Asleep' (a-prefix) is predicative-only; for an attributive say 'a sleeping rabbit'.
note.zh: asleep（a- 前缀）只作表语；作定语要说 a sleeping rabbit。

note.en: 'Happy' supplements the object 'rabbits' — an object complement.
note.zh: happy 补充宾语 rabbits——宾语补语。

examples.text: the rabbit came home tired and hungry

note.en: The adjective phrase 'tired and hungry' shows the rabbit's state on arriving — an adverbial.
note.zh: 形容词短语 tired and hungry 表现到家时的状态——作状语。

note.en: Adjective + present participle compound.
note.zh: 形容词加现在分词的复合。

note.en: Adverb + present participle compound (cf. 'all round', 'recently built').
note.zh: 副词加现在分词的复合（参 all round、recently built）。

note.en: Adjective + noun(+ed) = 'a man whose hair is white'.
note.zh: 形容词加名词(+ed) = a man whose hair is white。

note.en: Noun + adjective = 'a rabbit that is white like snow'.
note.zh: 名词加形容词 = a rabbit that is white like snow。

note.en: Noun + past participle = 'a rabbit whose heart is broken'.
note.zh: 名词加过去分词 = a rabbit whose heart is broken。

note.en: Regular -er / -est on a short adjective.
note.zh: 短形容词的规则 -er / -est。

note.en: Stressed closed syllable: double the final consonant.
note.zh: 重读闭音节：双写词尾辅音。

note.en: Multi-syllable adjective takes 'more' / 'most'.
note.zh: 多音节形容词用 more / most。

note.en: Irregular comparison (cf. bad/worse/worst, many/more/most, little/less/least).
note.zh: 不规则变化（参 bad/worse/worst、many/more/most、little/less/least）。

examples.text: this carrot is bigger than that one

note.en: Basic comparative pattern: A is + comparative + than + B.
note.zh: 基本比较级句型：A is 比较级 than B。

examples.text: this carrot is as big as that one

note.en: Equal comparison uses the positive degree inside 'as ... as'.
note.zh: 同级比较在 as ... as 中用原级。

examples.text: the rabbit is smarter and smarter

note.en: Repeated comparative = 'more and more': the rabbit gets smarter and smarter.
note.zh: 重复比较级 = 越来越：兔子越来越聪明。

examples.text: The more carrots the rabbit eats, the smarter he will become.

note.en: 'The + comparative, the + comparative' — cause then result (a hypothesis, so will-future).
note.zh: “the 比较级, the 比较级”——先因后果（表假设，用 will 将来）。

examples.text: who is the fairest one of all?

note.en: Superlative with 'of + group' as the range.
note.zh: 最高级配 of 加范围。

examples.text: Snow White is the most beautiful woman in the world.

note.en: Superlative takes 'the' (only one) plus 'in + place' as the range.
note.zh: 最高级加 the（仅一个）并用 in 加地点作范围。

examples.text: The queen is a most beautiful woman.

note.en: Here 'most' means 'very', so 'a' replaces 'the': = a very beautiful woman.
note.zh: 这里 most 表示“非常”，故用 a 而非 the：= a very beautiful woman。

prompt.en: Which ordering of pre-positioned adjectives sounds natural?
prompt.zh: 哪种前置形容词顺序听上去自然？

explain.en: Size comes before colour, so 'little' precedes 'white'; both go before the noun.
explain.zh: 尺寸在颜色之前，故 little 在 white 前；两者都在名词前。

prompt.en: In 'the rabbit is hungry', what role does 'hungry' play?
prompt.zh: 在 the rabbit is hungry 中，hungry 的成分是什么？

explain.en: After the linking verb 'is', 'hungry' supplements the subject — a subject complement.
explain.zh: 在系动词 is 之后，hungry 补充主语——主语补语。

prompt.en: Which adjective normally works ONLY as a predicative, not as a pre-positioned attributive?
prompt.zh: 哪个形容词通常只能作表语，不能作前置定语？

explain.en: A-prefix adjectives like 'asleep' are predicative-only: 'the rabbit is asleep', not 'an asleep rabbit'. Use 'sleeping' for an attributive.
explain.zh: asleep 这类 a- 前缀形容词只作表语：the rabbit is asleep，不能说 an asleep rabbit；作定语用 sleeping。

prompt.en: Write the comparative and superlative of 'big', separated by a comma.
prompt.zh: 写出 big 的比较级和最高级，用逗号隔开。

explain.en: A stressed closed syllable doubles the final consonant: big -> bigger -> biggest.
explain.zh: 重读闭音节双写词尾辅音：big -> bigger -> biggest。

prompt.en: Fill the blank: 'interesting, ____ interesting, most interesting'.

explain.en: Multi-syllable adjectives form the comparative with 'more', not '-er'.
explain.zh: 多音节形容词用 more 构成比较级，而非 -er。

prompt.en: Rewrite the compound 'a white haired man' as a relative clause: 'a man ____ hair is white'.
prompt.zh: 把复合形容词 a white haired man 改写为定语从句：a man ____ hair is white。

explain.en: Adjective + noun(+ed) compounds equal a 'whose' relative clause: a man whose hair is white.
explain.zh: 形容词加名词(+ed) 的复合等于 whose 定语从句：a man whose hair is white。

quizzes.sentence: I want to meet some nice somebody.
quizzes.fix: I want to meet somebody nice.

explain.en: With a compound indefinite pronoun like 'somebody', the adjective must come after it: 'somebody nice', not 'nice somebody'.
explain.zh: 修饰 somebody 这类复合不定代词时，形容词必须后置：somebody nice，而非 nice somebody。

quizzes.sentence: The rabbit is more and more prettier.
quizzes.fix: The rabbit is prettier and prettier.

explain.en: 'Pretty' forms its comparative with '-er', so use 'prettier and prettier'. Don't mix 'more' with the '-er' form.
explain.zh: pretty 用 -er 构成比较级，故用 prettier and prettier；不要把 more 和 -er 形式混用。

quizzes.sentence: This carrot is three times big as that one.
quizzes.fix: This carrot is three times as big as that one.

explain.en: Equal comparison needs 'as ... as' around the positive degree; the multiple goes before the first 'as'.
explain.zh: 同级比较需要用 as ... as 包住原级；倍数放在第一个 as 之前。

prompt.en: Why is it 'the most beautiful woman' but 'a most beautiful woman'?
prompt.zh: 为什么是 the most beautiful woman，却又有 a most beautiful woman？

text.en: Both mean the superlative 'most'; the article is interchangeable.
text.zh: 两者都是最高级 most，冠词可互换。

text.en: With 'the' it is the true superlative (only one); with 'a', 'most' means 'very'.
text.zh: 加 the 是真正最高级（仅一个）；加 a 时 most 表示“非常”。

text.en: 'A most' is simply a grammatical error.
text.zh: a most 只是语法错误。

explain.en: A superlative takes 'the' because there is only one; in 'a most beautiful woman', 'most' means 'very', so 'a' is correct: a very beautiful woman.
explain.zh: 最高级加 the 因为只有一个；在 a most beautiful woman 中 most 表示“非常”，故用 a：a very beautiful woman。

prompt.en: In 'carrots make rabbits happy', what role does 'happy' play?
prompt.zh: 在 carrots make rabbits happy 中，happy 的成分是什么？

explain.en: 'Happy' supplements the object 'rabbits', completing what the verb 'make' does to it — an object complement.
explain.zh: happy 补充宾语 rabbits，完成 make 对它的作用——宾语补语。

prompt.en: Complete the cause-and-result pattern (will-future): 'The more carrots the rabbit eats, the ____ he will become.' (smart)
prompt.zh: 补全因果句型（will 将来）：The more carrots the rabbit eats, the ____ he will become。（smart）

explain.en: 'The + comparative, the + comparative' links cause and result; 'smart' takes '-er' -> 'smarter'.
explain.zh: “the 比较级, the 比较级”连接因果；smart 加 -er -> smarter。

title.en: A rabbit shows off every adjective trick
title.zh: 兔子秀遍形容词各种用法

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: Look, a little white rabbit asleep over there.

note.en: Pre-positioned 'little white' (size before colour); 'asleep' post-positioned because a-prefix words resist the attributive slot.
note.zh: 前置 little white（尺寸在颜色前）；asleep 后置，因为 a- 前缀词难作前置定语。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: I am awake now, and I am hungry.

note.en: Linking verb + 'hungry' as subject complement (predicative).
note.zh: 系动词 + hungry 作主语补语（表语）。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: Carrots make rabbits happy, they say.

note.en: 'Happy' is the object complement of 'rabbits'.
note.zh: happy 是 rabbits 的宾语补语。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: This carrot is bigger than that one, so I will eat it first.

note.en: Comparative + 'than' compares two carrots.
note.zh: 比较级 + than 比较两根胡萝卜。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: The more carrots you eat, the smarter you become.

note.en: 'The + comparative, the + comparative' — cause and result.
note.zh: “the 比较级, the 比较级”——因果。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: Then one day I'll be the smartest rabbit in the world.

note.en: Superlative with 'the' and 'in + place' as the range.
note.zh: 最高级加 the，并用 in 加地点作范围。
